title: Release Reconciliation, Incident Response, and Production Defense · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A public-interest newsroom incrementally builds defensible production Go feed aggregator from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, and PostgreSQL fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP-client, HTTP-server, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of defensible production Go feed aggregator while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Bind reviewed revision, generated code, migrations, dependency graph, tests, image digest, SBOM, signature, configuration, and deployment identity.
Demonstrate unfamiliar RSS and Atom sources from authorized discovery through bounded fetch, normalization, transaction, scheduling, API, accessible reader, and cleanup.
Triage freshness, publisher overload, SSRF, parser, database, tenant, content, search, rollout, and restore incidents with evidence-preserving containment.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Release Chain
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 5356 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to defend source-to-artifact provenance, unfamiliar end-to-end behavior, incidents, recovery, and residual risk; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then change one artifact, deployment, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Bind reviewed revision, generated code, migrations, dependency graph, tests, image digest, SBOM, signature, configuration, and deployment identity.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Release Chain.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for bind reviewed revision, generated code, migrations, dependency graph, tests, image digest, sbom, signature, configuration, and deployment identity. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Release Chain, keep Feed Go Release Chain connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
